title_zh: 超越聊天机器人的直接偏好优化
summary_zh: 本文介绍了直接偏好优化（Direct Preference Optimization，DPO）技术如何应用于超越聊天机器人的更广泛场景，提升模型根据用户偏好进行调整的能力。
summary_en: The article introduces how Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) technology can be applied beyond chatbots to broader scenarios, enhancing models' ability to adjust based on user preferences.
why_it_matters_zh: 随着AI应用多样化，优化模型以更好地匹配用户偏好成为关键，DPO提供了一种直接且有效的方法。
why_it_matters_en: As AI applications diversify, optimizing models to better match user preferences is crucial, and DPO offers a direct and effective approach.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Direct Preference Optimization Beyond Chatbots. Category: tools_apps. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 用于大型语言模型结构化推理的视觉图形支架
title_en: Visual Graph Scaffolds for Structural Reasoning in Large Language Models
summary_zh: 本文探讨图形结构不仅作为外部知识源提供信息，更可以作为大型语言模型内部的推理辅助工具。通过在多跳问答任务中，将教师提供的推理轨迹重写为图形思维导图以指导学生模型，实验发现将图形结构扁平化为文本后，推理效率和答题质量显著下降；而视觉图形指导即使在没有直接答案提示时仍有效，并且优势在监督微调和基于KL的蒸馏后持续存在。
summary_en: This paper explores the use of graph structures not only to supply information but also as an internal reasoning aid for large language models (LLMs). On multi-hop question answering tasks, teacher-provided reasoning traces are converted into graph mind maps to guide a student model. Experiments show that flattening graph structures into text reduces reasoning efficiency and answer quality, especially without direct answer hints. In contrast, visual graph guidance remains effective without direct clues and maintains its advantage after supervised fine-tuning and KL-based distillation.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究指出图形结构应被视为组织推理的视觉支架，而不仅是外部知识来源，促进了对大型语言模型内部推理机制的理解和提升。
why_it_matters_en: The study highlights that graphs serve as visual scaffolds organizing reasoning internally rather than just external knowledge sources, advancing understanding and performance of reasoning in large language models.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Visual Graph Scaffolds for Structural Reasoning in Large Language Models. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: AURA：针对机器人策略的动作门控记忆，实现恒定显存占用
title_en: AURA: Action-Gated Memory for Robot Policies at Constant VRAM
summary_zh: AURA-Mem（动作效用循环自适应记忆）是一种专为机器人设计的记忆机制，解决了边缘设备带宽有限、高带宽存储和闪存耐用性不足的问题。它通过固定大小的循环记忆和学习门控，仅在当前观察会影响下一动作时写入记忆，从而实现了无论执行时间多长，显存占用固定。测试中，AURA-Mem在准确率相当的情况下，写入次数比传统方法减少5倍多，且在真实机器人任务中保持成功率不降反升，显著提高了写入效率和显存利用率。
summary_en: AURA-Mem (Action-Utility Recurrent Adaptive Memory) targets the constraints of robot policy memory on bandwidth-limited edge hardware, where computation is not the main bottleneck but memory writes are. It employs a constant-size recurrent memory and a learned gate that only writes when current observations would alter the next action, maintaining a fixed inference state size regardless of episode length. Experimentally, AURA-Mem matches baseline accuracy with significantly fewer writes and improved memory efficiency, while maintaining or surpassing success rates in closed-loop robotic tasks.
why_it_matters_zh: 传统的KV缓存适合数据中心的短批量推理，但不适合机器人连续长时任务，因其内存会随着步数大幅增长。AURA通过动作门控记忆降低写入频率和显存占用，解决了机器人边缘设备受限资源的问题，提高了记忆使用效率和执行效能。
why_it_matters_en: KV caches suit datacenter inference with many short, reset requests, but are inefficient for robots running long, continuous episodes on resource-limited edge hardware. AURA's action-gated memory reduces unnecessary writes and keeps memory usage constant, addressing hardware constraints and enhancing robotic policy effectiveness.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: AURA: Action-Gated Memory for Robot Policies at Constant VRAM. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 评估Transformer与LSTM框架在无测站流域预测中的表现
title_en: Evaluating Transformer and LSTM Frameworks for Prediction in Ungauged Basins
summary_zh: 本研究使用美国国家水模型的回溯模拟，比较了编码器结构的Transformer与LSTM在缺乏水文信息的无测站流域中对上游径流预测的表现。结果显示，在仅使用上游信息以及结合上下游信息的情况下，LSTM整体性能优于Transformer。加入下游信息后，所有模型的预测技能大幅提升，中位数NNSE提升超过60%。研究表明，循环记忆机制更适合处理此类上游径流重建任务，而下游水文背景作为辅助约束显著增强了各架构的预测能力。
summary_en: This study compares an encoder-only Transformer and LSTM models using retrospective simulations from the NOAA National Water Model for upstream streamflow prediction in ungauged basins with limited hydrologic data. Results show that LSTM outperforms Transformer in both upstream-only and combined upstream-downstream configurations. Incorporating downstream information significantly improves prediction, increasing median NNSE by over 60%. Findings suggest recurrent memory aligns better with upstream reconstruction tasks, while downstream hydrologic context serves as a strong auxiliary constraint enhancing prediction across architectures.
why_it_matters_zh: 无测站流域缺乏观测数据，增加预测的不确定性。本研究通过比较两种常用深度学习框架，探讨了其水文序列推断的有效性与结构偏好，为水文预测模型选择提供实践依据。结果强调了循环神经网络在捕捉时间序列信息上的优势，以及利用下游信息提升无测站流域预测技能的重要性。
why_it_matters_en: Ungauged basins lack direct observations, complicating prediction efforts. This study compares Transformer and LSTM frameworks to assess their effectiveness and architectural suitability for hydrologic sequence inference. Results highlight the advantages of recurrent neural networks in temporal data reconstruction and underscore the value of incorporating downstream hydrologic context to improve prediction skill in ungauged basins.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Evaluating Transformer and LSTM Frameworks for Prediction in Ungauged Basins. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 比人类更环保？大型语言模型中的环境态度
title_en: Greener Than Humans? Environmental Attitudes in Large Language Models
summary_zh: 本文开发了一个评估大型语言模型（LLMs）环境认知、情感及行为推荐的基准，并测试了31个广泛使用的模型。结果显示，许多LLMs表现出比德国普通调查受访者更环保的态度和行为建议，倾向于具有显著减碳潜力的行动。然而，模型的环保回应与其起源、规模或发布背景无明显关联，但受提示语境和角色设定影响显著，存在迎合用户意识形态倾向的风险。研究强调了在AI系统广泛应用于可持续发展与公共决策时，治理、透明和监督的重要性。
summary_en: This paper develops a benchmark to evaluate environmental cognition, affect, and behavioral recommendations in 31 widely used large language models (LLMs). It finds that many LLMs align more closely with environmentally progressive attitudes than average German survey respondents, recommending behaviors with potential for significant CO2 reduction. No clear link is found between sustainability responses and model origin, size, or release context. However, models show contextual sensitivity controlled by persona-based prompting and tend to mirror user ideological positions, raising concerns about steerability and normative reliability. The study highlights the need for governance, transparency, and oversight as AI becomes embedded in sustainability and public decision-making.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究首次系统量化评估LLMs的环境价值取向，发现它们普遍表现出较强环保意识和行动建议，这对未来AI在可持续发展中的应用具有重要启示。同时，模型的意识形态敏感性提醒我们在实际部署时需加强规范和监管，确保AI输出的可靠性与公正性。
why_it_matters_en: The study provides the first systematic quantitative assessment of LLMs' environmental value alignment, revealing their strong pro-environmental attitudes and behavioral recommendations, which is crucial for AI's role in sustainability. Additionally, the models' ideological sensitivity underscores the need for governance and oversight to ensure reliability and fairness in real-world deployment.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Greener Than Humans? Environmental Attitudes in Large Language Models. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 大语言模型中词汇性持久影响的研究
title_en: On the Persistent Effects of Lexicality in Large Language Models
summary_zh: 来自arXiv的新研究探讨了大语言模型（LLMs）中提取的表示结构，发现其往往受词汇重叠影响多于语义内容。研究通过对抗性语义测试及信息论视角，量化了词汇影响与语义内容的关系。结果显示词汇影响贯穿模型深度，无论架构、训练方式及目标函数，甚至在专注语义相似度的模型中均存在。同时，发现模型中间深度区域词汇和语义信号同时减弱，表明该段表示难以有效捕捉表面形式和意义。论文还通过摘要和模型编辑案例，展示了词汇影响对下游任务的影响。
summary_en: A new study from arXiv investigates how representations extracted from large language models (LLMs) are influenced more by lexical overlap than semantic content. Using adversarial semantic stress tests and an information theory perspective, the authors quantify the relationship between lexical influence and semantic information. Their findings reveal that lexical influence extends through model depth consistently across architectures, training regimes, and objectives, including models trained for semantic similarity. They identify a mid-depth layer region where both lexical and semantic signals degrade simultaneously, indicating poor representation of both surface form and meaning. Case studies on summarization and model editing demonstrate lexical influence effects on downstream applications.
why_it_matters_zh: 理解大语言模型中词汇性对表示的持久影响，有助于改进模型设计与训练策略，从而提升语义理解和下游任务性能。
why_it_matters_en: Understanding the persistent lexical influence in LLM representations can inform better architecture and training choices, ultimately improving semantic understanding and downstream task effectiveness.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: On the Persistent Effects of Lexicality in Large Language Models. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 主题作为社会人口统计代理：对话上下文如何影响大型语言模型回答
title_en: Topics as Proxies for Sociodemographics: How Conversational Context Affects LLM Answers
summary_zh: 本文研究表明，大型语言模型（LLMs）难以仅凭单次对话历史推断用户的社会人口统计特征。尽管不同群体间的结果存在差异，但差异幅度有限。通过分析对话主题、情绪和易读性等语言特征，发现对话主题是预测LLM生成建议的最主要因素，这些主题在一定程度上代替了社会人口统计群体的作用，且往往以不可预测的方式影响建议。这提示在高风险应用场景下，应关注对话上下文对LLM输出的影响并进行研究和干预。
summary_en: This study shows that large language models (LLMs) struggle to infer user sociodemographics from a single conversation history, with minimal magnitude differences across groups. By analyzing various (psycho)linguistic features such as conversation topics, emotions, and readability, the research finds that conversation topics are the strongest predictors of LLM-generated advice. These topics serve as proxies for sociodemographic groups and can unpredictably influence advice. This raises concerns for high-stakes scenarios, highlighting the need for further research to understand and mitigate conversational context effects on LLM outputs.
why_it_matters_zh: 因为对话主题作为间接的社会人口统计指标，会以不可预测的方式影响LLM输出，这在涉及法律、医疗等重大决策的场景中可能带来公平性问题，提醒我们需深入研究与调控。
why_it_matters_en: Because conversation topics indirectly proxy sociodemographics and unpredictably affect LLM outputs, this can raise fairness concerns in critical domains like law and medicine, urging deeper investigation and control of such context effects.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Topics as Proxies for Sociodemographics: How Conversational Context Affects LLM Answers. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
